MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jeffrey B. Workman; 415-581-0885; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Anna Estrada and cross-defendant Timothy Joseph Workman.
Conrad K. Wu; 415-581-0885; appearing on behalf of cross-defendant Edward Higginbotham.
Sally Liu; 415-635-1783; plaintiff and cross-complainant in pro per, appearing on their own behalf.

Hearing held with all parties present in the courtroom. Mr. Wu and Mr. Workman contest the denied portion of the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Liu contests that granted portion of the tentative ruling. The court orders that the unauthorized supplemental briefs filed by plaintiff on November 17, 2021 are hereby stricken and will not be considered. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Claims based on the following allegations are stricken "cross-defendants filed a groundless lawsuit.. orally uttered false allegations concerning the content of the complaint filed an opposition.. to demurrer and requested sanctions," including claims for abuse of process, malicious prosecution, conspiracy, intentional infliction of emotional distress, UCL, 8-HUD Fraud arising out of the above-quoted allegations. Cross-Complaint failed to establish probability of prevailing on the merits with competent admissible evidence; also CC 47(b). Motion is denied as to the claim for fraud, trespass, violation of rent ordinance (nuisance) health code, quiet title and partition. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and identify claims arising out of protected activity.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
